concerns.text: Tasks T016-T018 are marked as 'FAILED' or 'rejected' in the task list, yet they are the ONLY mechanism defined to resolve the critical contradiction between the Spec (FR-001/FR-002: 1000/50 samples) and the Plan (150/150 Balanced Blocked Design). Without these tasks being marked 'completed' and the Spec updated, the implementation tasks (T012, T013) violate the 'Single Source of Truth' (Constitution Principle IV) by implementing a design explicitly forbidden by the current active Spec. The task list cannot proceed with implementation while the Spec/Plan conflict remains unresolved in the artifact itself.

Task T019 ('Contract test for PMD wrapper') is marked as unchecked/missing, yet it is listed as a mandatory prerequisite for T020 and the US2 implementation. The Spec (FR-004) and Plan require static analysis execution; without the contract test defining the interface, the implementation task T021 lacks a verified interface definition, violating the 'Tests are MANDATORY' constraint stated in the tasks.md header and the 'Reproducibility' principle (Constitution I) which requires deterministic interfaces.

Multiple implementation tasks (T012, T014, T021, T023, T027, T032) contain '[UNRESOLVED-CLAIM]' annotations. These tasks claim to satisfy specific FRs (e.g., T012 satisfies FR-001 sample collection) but explicitly admit the underlying logic or thresholds are 'not_enough_info' or 'refuted'. This constitutes a semantic weakening of the requirements: a task cannot satisfy an FR if its own description admits the implementation details are undefined or the claims are refuted. This violates the 'No weakening of FRs' constraint.
